blurb: Drill in to work on proposal-draft review threads: anchoring, resolution, regenerate carry-forward, the auto-addressed sweep, or the content-drift 'Not a real drift' dismissal.
intent: Document the inline review-comment system layered on a proposal run: how a feedback thread is anchored (general / topic field / proposal summary / wiki range), the thread kinds (human comment, agent review_note, agent content_drift note), what HTTP endpoints CRUD threads and comments, how the ORM stores them with revision pointers, how open threads are formatted into the next regenerate prompt, how the auto-`addressed` sweep snaps a thread closed when its anchored content changes between revisions (diffing a wiki_range note against the anchored topic's OWN per-topic wiki so a scoped regenerate doesn't close untouched topics' notes), and the 'Not a real drift' escape hatch that re-baselines a topic's ref digests and dismisses its content-drift signal on both the origin-run note and the standalone fallback proposal so it stops re-firing.
label: Proposal review comments (feedback threads anchored to topics & wiki, incl. content-drift notes)
